expectations.text: Output contains a properly structured search matrix with Context, Tier 1, Tier 2, and Tier 3 sections
expectations.evidence: All three tiers present with proper markdown table headers and a Context section with Goal, Type, Domain

expectations.text: Matrix uses at least 3 different engine categories including legal or policy databases
expectations.evidence: Uses Google, Google Scholar, Semantic Scholar, YouTube, GitHub, and targets legal domains via site: filters (eur-lex.europa.eu, nist.gov, iapp.org)

expectations.text: Search queries use engine-specific operators such as site:, filetype:pdf, or date range filters
expectations.evidence: Includes site:eur-lex.europa.eu, site:nist.gov, filetype:pdf, site:iapp.org, after:2023, and year:2023-2026

expectations.text: Tier 2 queries broaden scope compared to Tier 1, and Tier 3 explores alternative or lateral sources
expectations.evidence: Tier 1 targets primary regulatory texts; Tier 2 adds enforcement case studies and risk classification; Tier 3 explores compliance tooling and video content

expectations.text: Each row includes specific acceptance criteria for evaluating search results
expectations.evidence: All 10 rows have an Acceptance column with specific criteria

expectations.text: Output includes a Runtime Recovery section with fallback strategies
expectations.evidence: Runtime Recovery section contains 4 bullet points including fallbacks for EUR-Lex, NIST, and China translation sources

expectations.text: Matrix covers regulatory frameworks from at least EU, US, and China
expectations.evidence: Tier 1 rows 1-3 explicitly target EU AI Act, NIST AI RMF, and China Interim Measures respectively

expectations.text: At least one query specifically targets enforcement actions or audit case studies
expectations.evidence: Tier 2 row 1 queries for 'LLM regulatory audit case study compliance enforcement action'

expectations.text: Output includes a Grading Summary section with per-tier pass/fail thresholds
expectations.evidence: Grading Summary is present but the Tier 2 threshold rationale could be more specific about enforcement data requirements
